title: MANGA NGE Eva Manga Action Ed 2 Alternate Soundtrack
display_title: MANGA NGE Eva Manga Action Ed 2 Alternate Soundtrack
wikitext:
[]: [NGE] Eva Manga Action Ed. 2 Alternate Soundtrack             Quote Post by houtaru » Tue Jun 22, 2004 11:02 pm Have any of you read the alternate soundtrack listing by Carl Gustav in the new edtion of volume 2? It's a decent listing of songs that go with each episode of the anime. However, I can think better choices he could have made. What are your thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jun 23, 2004 12:53 pm I broke it out this morning and typed it up. http://www.evamonkey.com/alternate_eva_soundtrack.htm I don't really agree with his choices. But then again, I'm not really into many of the artists he's listed. I'm aware of them, I'm just not into many of them, aside from perhaps Smashing Pumpkins, Nine Inch Nails, and a couple others. Its tough to pick a single sound to encapsulate an entire episode. If anything, I'd like to pick his choices for character themes. Maybe he did for later volumes. There's an alternative soundtrack for The End of Evangelion in volume 3. I'll type that up as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Wed Jun 23, 2004 1:11 pm I honestly couldn't disagree more with his choices. I would rather hear classical/instrumental music with actual meaning then hear some metal idiot yelling his head off, or whatever type of music you would say Nine Inch Nails or Smashing Pumpkins produces.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jun 23, 2004 4:29 pm As promised, here is the alternate soundtrack to The End of Evangelion, found in the viz second edition of volume 3 of the Evangelion manga. http://www.evamonkey.com/alternate_eoe_soundtrack.htm

[]:             Quote Post by houtaru » Thu Jun 24, 2004 12:44 am Thank you for making a list of those. I haven't bought volume 3 yet. I listened to all the music and most of the tracks are horrible. The Smashing Pumpkins I agree with, as someone else said. Gustav put Coldplay's The Scientist to episode 23 becuase he said it reminded him of Ristko. I don't agree. As I listen to the lyrics, It reminds me of Gendo and Yui.

[]:             Quote Post by the-artist-known-as-chris » Sat Jul 24, 2004 11:24 pm Phaze wrote: I honestly couldn't disagree more with his choices. I would rather hear classical/instrumental music with actual meaning then hear some metal idiot yelling his head off, or whatever type of music you would say Nine Inch Nails or Smashing Pumpkins produces.

[]:             Quote Post by Esion » Sun Jul 25, 2004 3:06 am Those have got to be some of the worst choices for Eva I have ever seen. I wouldn't say that even one of those songs goes well in my opinion.

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka Langley » Sun Jul 25, 2004 4:36 am It's true, some of the songs are unfitting, and I laugh just thinking about them being in Eva at those parts. But it's definately a north americanized way of seeing it.

[]:             Quote Post by MongolSquad » Sun Jul 25, 2004 10:22 am Some of those bands shouldn't be in the same sentence, or even same paragraph as Eva. I for one have the EoE OST which is teh r0x0r. I don't why the would release a "revised" soundtrack. Probably to Amercanize and mainstream Eva just a little more. (aka "basterdization") >.<

[]:             Quote Post by Asuka Langley » Sun Jul 25, 2004 1:01 pm cain luppus wrote: Some of those bands shouldn't be in the same sentence, or even same paragraph as Eva. I for one have the EoE OST which is teh r0x0r. I don't why the would release a "revised" soundtrack. Probably to Amercanize and mainstream Eva just a little more. (aka "basterdization") >.<

[]:             Quote Post by lyricaldanichan » Tue Aug 31, 2004 4:21 am Asuka Langley wrote: cain luppus wrote: Some of those bands shouldn't be in the same sentence, or even same paragraph as Eva. I for one have the EoE OST which is teh r0x0r. I don't why the would release a "revised" soundtrack. Probably to Amercanize and mainstream Eva just a little more. (aka "basterdization") >.<

[]:             Quote Post by Phaze » Tue Aug 31, 2004 8:33 pm It's more the type of music suggested, then the content of the music itself that I have a problem with.

[]: I have to go with that...             Quote Post by Olin of Xephon » Tue Aug 31, 2004 9:43 pm I have nothing against (most) of the songs, I just don't fel much of a connection between them or Evanglion, it's not that I haven't tried it's just the two really don't fit together.

[]:             Quote Post by lyricaldanichan » Wed Sep 01, 2004 12:28 am Hmm that is a suprised that you couldn't connect with those songs because at the time a lot of people were able to. Is users on this thread born in the 80's or early 90's? I guess I am suprised of the reaction, but figured that might be the reason why nobody here understands. Not like this alt soundtrack is set in stone, it was Carl's view of what he felt would fit with the series.

[]:             Quote Post by Super Mega Ultra Deluxe » Wed Sep 01, 2004 1:35 am Mmmm, Sneaker Pimps But yeah, the list is a bit too personalized and even though I like some of those bands and songs, I don't think music like that goes well with Evangelion. I think any band music ing general wouldn't really work. Not condemning his choices though, it works for him. A custom soundtrack of music without lyrics or classical pieces would probably work better in my opinion.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Sep 01, 2004 11:18 am 08: Asuka STRIKES! love rollercoaster red hot chili peppers 11: THE DAY TOKYO-3 STOOD STILL 6 underground sneaker pimps

[]:             Quote Post by lyricaldanichan » Wed Sep 01, 2004 1:50 pm Super Mega Ultra Deluxe wrote: A custom soundtrack of music without lyrics or classical pieces would probably work better in my opinion.

[]:             Quote Post by Super Mega Ultra Deluxe » Wed Sep 01, 2004 8:03 pm lyricaldanichan wrote: There is allready classical pieces in the official soundtrack! LOL

[]:             Quote Post by houtaru » Wed Sep 01, 2004 11:14 pm So anyone want to make their own list for each episode? Could be fun^^. I'm always on the lookout for great music i've never heard before, epecially classical.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Thu Sep 02, 2004 11:50 pm I would rather hear classical/instrumental music with actual meaning then hear some metal idiot yelling his head off, or whatever type of music you would say Nine Inch Nails or Smashing Pumpkins produces.
